findings.evidence: `code/TauCeti/Algebra/AlgebraicGroup/HopfMap.lean:119` has `lemma mapValue_mapDomain (φ : H₁ →ₐc[R] H₂) (χ : A →ₐ[R] B)` with the same statement; `DiagonalizableGroupFunctoriality.lean:75-77` defines `pointsMap φ := AlgHom.mapDomain (MonoidAlgebra.mapDomainBialgHom R φ)`, and line 142 proves the theorem by exact application of that lemma.
findings.fix: Remove this theorem and use `AlgHom.mapValue_mapDomain (MonoidAlgebra.mapDomainBialgHom R φ) χ` directly, or justify why a diagonalizable-specific alias is needed.
findings.issue: `pointsMap_mapValue` is just the monoid-algebra specialization of the existing generic `AlgHom.mapValue_mapDomain`, creating a parallel API for the same commuting square.

summary: The new functoriality mostly specializes existing Hopf-map and group-algebra APIs appropriately, but it adds one parallel naturality theorem that is exactly the existing generic commuting-square lemma instantiated to monoid algebras.
